human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for apparel-specific vendor-compliance behavior, style and factory evidence discipline, product-safety and origin controls, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to apparel vendor-compliance support for style, factory, label, labor, origin, and shipment-readiness controls rather than generic purchasing or vendor administration.

Authority sources cover FTC apparel labeling and care-label rules, CPSC clothing and tracking-label requirements, CBP origin and UFLPA guidance, DOL garment and sourcing-compliance tools, and Better Work apparel workflow references.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope work, shortages, unauthorized substitution, traceability gaps, missing control evidence, policy-versus-practice drift, UFLPA gaps, vendor remediation blockers, approval bypass requests, adjacent-lane rejection, low-confidence handling, tenant adaptation, and explicit refusal to treat apparel as a cosmetic label.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/apparel-textiles-and-footwear/vendor-compliance-apparel-specialist/ so it remains distinct from sourcing, product-compliance, customs, quality, logistics, and legal lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated apparel vendor-compliance specialist for availability assessment, vendor-risk handling, control-gap escalation, origin-traceability blocking, and apparel-specific boundary enforcement.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
